message:
Add Tantivy search engine scaffolding

Establish search module structure with placeholder tantivy.rs for
future full-text search index implementation.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 3.1, § 5):
The search layer provides sub-50ms "search as you type" query latency
over all normalized conversation messages using Tantivy, a Lucene-like
full-text search engine written in Rust. This is the primary query
path for interactive TUI search; SQLite FTS5 serves as fallback when
Tantivy disabled via config or during index rebuild.

Planned tantivy.rs responsibilities:
- Schema definition: Define Tantivy index schema per PLAN § 5.2 with
  fields: message_id (u64, stored), conversation_id (u64, stored),
  agent_slug (string, indexed + fast field for term filters),
  workspace (string, indexed), created_at (i64, fast field for range
  queries and date sorting), role (string, indexed), title (text,
  indexed + stored, weight 3.0 in multi-field query), content (text,
  indexed + stored, weight 1.0). Use tantivy::schema::Schema builder
  with TEXT fields configured for standard tokenization (lowercase,
  stopword removal, stemming via tantivy::tokenizer::SimpleTokenizer
  and tantivy::tokenizer::Stemmer for English) and FAST fields for
  efficient filtering without full inverted index scan.

- Index initialization: Create on-disk index directory under app data
  path (e.g., ~/.local/share/coding-agent-search/index/v1/) with
  schema version subdirectory for future migration support. Acquire
  IndexWriter with 50MB heap budget (tantivy::Index::writer parameter)
  and single writer thread (no concurrent indexing, sequential batch
  writes from indexer thread). Open IndexReader with reload policy
  (OnCommit or manual) for TUI search queries via Searcher handle.

- Indexing operations: Batch document insertion—for each Message row
  from SQLite, build tantivy::Document with schema field values,
  accumulate ~1000 docs in memory buffer, then IndexWriter::add_document
  and periodic commit() every N messages (configurable, default 10k)
  to balance indexing throughput vs query freshness. Support incremental
  updates: delete documents by message_id term query before re-adding
  (Tantivy has no native UPDATE, so delete+insert for modified messages).

- Query construction: Implement search-as-you-type query builder that
  parses user input string into tantivy::query::BooleanQuery combining:
  (1) Multi-field full-text query on title (weight 3.0) and content
  (weight 1.0) using QueryParser with OR default operator for broad
  recall (typing "api refactor" matches docs with either word, BM25
  scoring ranks docs with both higher). (2) Optional TermQuery filters
  on agent_slug fast field for per-agent filtering (e.g., show only
  Codex conversations). (3) Optional RangeQuery on created_at fast
  field for time-bounded search (e.g., last 7 days). All filters
  combined with AND logic (must match text AND agent AND time range).

- Search execution: Use Searcher::search with TopDocs collector limiting
  to 100 results (per PLAN § 5.3) sorted by BM25 score descending,
  extract stored fields (message_id, conversation_id, title snippet,
  content snippet with match highlighting via tantivy::snippet::Snippet
  generator), return to TUI for results pane display. Async-compatible:
  spawn rayon thread pool task for search to avoid blocking tokio
  runtime, send results back via crossbeam channel.

- Performance optimizations: Pre-open IndexReader at application startup
  (via tantivy::Index::reader()) and reuse across queries to avoid
  per-search overhead of segment metadata loading. Leverage Tantivy's
  internal thread pool (defaults to num_cpus for query execution) plus
  rayon for post-processing (grouping results by conversation_id,
  deduplicating, selecting best-scoring message per conversation for
  TUI display per PLAN § 5.3 grouping requirement). Debounce keystroke-
  triggered searches in UI layer by ~100–150ms: send SearchRequest on
  each input change to channel, worker deduplicates by dropping stale
  requests if newer one pending.

Index storage trade-offs:
Tantivy index size typically 30-50% of source text size (compressed
inverted lists, shared term dictionaries). For baseline corpus (10k
conversations, 1M messages, ~500MB raw text per PLAN § 12.3), expect
~150-250MB index. This is small enough to keep in OS page cache on
modern systems (8GB+ RAM), enabling <50ms cold query latency target.
If index grows large (100k+ conversations), consider index segmentation
by date range or per-agent indexes merged at query time.

Why Tantivy over Lucene/Elasticsearch:
- Native Rust integration: no JVM runtime dependency, simpler
  deployment (single binary goal per PLAN § 1.1 portability)
- Embeddable: library not a server, eliminates network latency and
  separate process management
- Performance parity: Tantivy's BM25 implementation and segment merge
  policies comparable to Lucene for this workload (mostly append-only
  with periodic bulk reindexing, not high-velocity updates)
- Smaller footprint: Tantivy query executor uses less memory than
  Elasticsearch for equivalent corpus size, critical for CLI tool that
  may run alongside multiple coding agents and IDEs

This commit contains only module declaration (mod.rs) and empty
placeholder file (tantivy.rs) to establish the search/ namespace per
implementation checklist (PLAN § 14.2: core modules). Actual schema
definition and indexing logic will be implemented after storage layer
provides conversation data to index and TUI layer demonstrates query
input/display flow (PLAN § 14.3-14.7 order).

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (search
component), § 5 (Tantivy design), § 5.4 (performance targets),
Cargo.toml (tantivy dependency), README.md (search/ module description).
